volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Andromache (Section 340 - Section 530b)

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: τέκνου θανόντος ῥᾳδίως ἀνέξεται ;
sections.translation: brook the murder of his child ? Troy has no such coward ’ s tale to tell of him ; nay , he will follow duty ’ s call ; his actions will prove him a worthy scion of Peleus and Achilles . Thy daughter will he thrust forth from his house ; and what wilt thou

sections.label: Section 345
sections.original: ἄλλῳ τί λέξεις ; πότερον ὡς κακὸν πόσιν
sections.translation: say when seeking to betroth her to another ? wilt say her virtue made her leave a worthless lord ? Nay , that will be false . Who then will wed her ? wilt thou keep her without a husband in thy halls , grown grey in widowhood ? Unhappy wretch ! dost not see the flood-gates of trouble opening wide for thee ?

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: πόσας ἂν εὐνὰς θυγατέρ ’ ἠδικημένην
sections.translation: How many a wrong against a wife wouldst thou prefer thy daughter to have found to suffering what I now describe ? We ought not on trifling grounds to promote serious mischief ; nor should men , if we women are so deadly a curse , bring their nature down to our level .

sections.label: Section 355
sections.original: ἡμεῖς γὰρ εἰ σὴν παῖδα φαρμακεύομεν
sections.translation: No ! if , as thy daughter asserts , I am practising sorcery against her and making her barren , right willingly will I , without any crouching at altars , submit in my own person to the penalty that lies in her husband ’ s hands ,

sections.label: Section 360
sections.original: βλάβην ὀφείλω προστιθεῖσ ’ ἀπαιδίαν .
sections.translation: seeing that I am no less chargeable with injuring him if I make him childless . This is my case ; but for thee , there is one thing [*] I fear in thy disposition ; it was a quarrel for a woman that really induced thee to destroy poor Ilium ’ s town .

sections.label: Section 364
sections.original: ἄγαν ἔλεξας ὡς γυνὴ πρὸς ἄρσενας ,
sections.translation: Thou hast said too much for a woman speaking to men . that discretion hath shot away its last shaft from thy soul ’ s quiver . [*]

sections.label: Section 366
sections.original: γύναι , τάδ ’ ἐστὶ σμικρὰ καὶ μοναρχίας
sections.translation: Woman , these are petty matters , unworthy , as thou sayest , of my despotic sway , unworthy too of Hellas . Yet mark this well ; his special fancy of the hour is of more moment to a man than Troy ’ s capture .

sections.label: Section 370
sections.original: κἀγὼ θυγατρί — μεγάλα γὰρ κρίνω τάδε ,
sections.translation: I then have set myself to help my daughter because I consider her loss of a wife ’ s rights a grave matter ; for whatever else a woman suffers is secondary to this ; if she loses her husband ’ s love she loses her life therewith . Now , as it is right Neoptolemus should rule my slaves ,

sections.label: Section 375
sections.original: καὶ τῶν ἐκείνου τοὺς ἐμούς , ἡμᾶς τε πρός ·
sections.translation: so my friends and I should have control of his ; for friends , if they be really friends , keep nothing to themselves , but have all in common . So if I wait for the absent instead of making the best arrangement I can at once of my affairs , I show weakness , not wisdom .

sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: ἀλλ ’ ἐξανίστω τῶνδ ’ ἀνακτόρων θεᾶς ·
sections.translation: Arise then , leave the goddess ’ s shrine , for by thy death this child escapeth his , whereas , if thou refuse to die , I will slay him ; for one of you twain must perish .

sections.label: Section 384
sections.original: οἴμοι , πικρὰν κλήρωσιν αἵρεσίν τέ μοι
sections.translation: Ah me ! ’ tis a bitter lot thou art offering about my

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: βίου καθίστης · καὶ λαχοῦσά γ ’ ἀθλία
sections.translation: life ; whether I take it or not I am equally unfortunate . Attend to me , thou who for a trifling cause art committing an awful crime . Why art thou bent on slaying me ? What reason hast thou ? What city have I betrayed ? Which of thy children was ever slain by me ?

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: ποῖον δ ’ ἔπρησα δῶμ ’; ἐκοιμήθην βίᾳ
sections.translation: What house have I fired ? I was forced to be my master ’ s concubine ; and spite of that wilt thou slay me , not him who is to blame , passing by the cause and hurrying to the inevitable result ? Ah me ! my sorrows ! Woe for my hapless country !

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: ὡς δεινὰ πάσχω . τί δέ με καὶ τεκεῖν ἐχρῆν
sections.translation: How cruel my fate ! Why had I to be a mother too and take upon me a double load of suffering ? Yet why do I mourn the past , and o ’ er the present never shed [*] a tear or compute its griefs ? I that saw Hector butchered and dragged behind the chariot ,

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: κατεῖδον οἰκτρῶς τ ’ Ἴλιον πυρούμενον ,
sections.translation: and Ilium , piteous sight ! one sheet of flame , while I was haled away by the hair of my head to the Argive ships in I slavery , and on my arrival in Phthia was assigned to Hector ’ s murderer as his mistress . What pleasure then has life for me ? Whither am I to turn my gaze ?

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: πρὸς τὰς παρούσας ἢ παρελθούσας τύχας ;
sections.translation: to the present or the past ? My babe alone was left me , the light of my life ; and him these ministers of death would slay . No ! they shall not , if my poor life can save him ; for if he be saved , hope in him lives on ,

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: ἐμοὶ δ ’ ὄνειδος μὴ θανεῖν ὑπὲρ τέκνου .
sections.translation: while to ’ twere shame to refuse to die for my son . Lo ! here I leave the altar and give myself into your hands , to cut or stab , to bind or hang . Ah ! my child , to Hades now thy mother passes to save thy dear life . Yet if thou escape thy doom ,

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: μέμνησο μητρός , οἷα τλᾶσ ’ ἀπωλόμην ,
sections.translation: remember me , my sufferings and my death , and tell thy father how I fared , with fond caress and streaming eye and arms thrown round his neck . Ah ! yes , his children are to every man as his own soul ; and [*] whoso sneers at this through inexperience ,

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: ἧσσον μὲν ἀλγεῖ , δυστυχῶν δ ’ εὐδαιμονεῖ .
sections.translation: though he suffers less anguish , yet tastes the bitter in his cup of bliss .

sections.label: Section 421
sections.original: ᾤκτιρ ’ ἀκούσασ ’· οἰκτρὰ γὰρ τὰ δυστυχῆ
sections.translation: Thy tale with pity fills me ; for every man alike , stranger though he be , feels pity for another ’ s distress . Menelaus , ’ tis thy duty to reconcile thy daughter and this captive , giving her a respite from sorrow .

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: λάβεσθέ μοι τῆσδ ’, ἀμφελίξαντες χέρας ,
sections.translation: Ho ! sirrahs , catch me this woman ; hold her fast ; for ’ tis no welcome story she will have to hear . It was to make thee leave the holy altar of the goddess that I held thy child ’ s death before thy eyes , and so induced thee to give thyself up to me to die .

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: καὶ τἀμφὶ σοῦ μὲν ὧδ ’ ἔχοντ ’ ἐπίστασο ·
sections.translation: So stands thy case , be well assured ; but as for this child , my daughter shall decide whether she will slay him or no . Get thee hence into the house , and there learn to bridle thy insolence in speaking to the free , slave that thou art .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: οἴμοι · δόλῳ μ ’ ὑπῆλθες , ἠπατήμεθα .
sections.translation: Alas ! thou hast by treachery beguiled me ; I was deceived .

sections.label: Section 436
sections.original: κήρυσσ ’ ἅπασιν · οὐ γὰρ ἐξαρνούμεθα .
sections.translation: Proclaim it to the world ; I do not deny it

sections.label: Section 437
sections.original: ἦ ταῦτ ’ ἐν ὑμῖν τοῖς παρ ’ Εὐρώτᾳ σοφά ;
sections.translation: Is this counted cleverness amongst you who dwell by the Eurotas ?

sections.label: Section 438
sections.original: καὶ τοῖς γε Τροίᾳ , τοὺς παθόντας ἀντιδρᾶν .
sections.translation: Yes , and amongst Trojans too , that those who suffer should retaliate .

sections.label: Section 439
sections.original: τὰ θεῖα δ ’ οὐ θεῖ ’ οὐδ ’ ἔχειν ἡγῇ δίκην ;
sections.translation: Thinkest thou God ’ s hand is shortened , and that thou wilt not be punished ?

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: ὅταν τάδ ’ ᾖ , τότ ’ οἴσομεν · σὲ δὲ κτενῶ .
sections.translation: Whene ’ er that comes , I am ready to bear it . But thy life will I have .

sections.label: Section 441
sections.original: ἦ καὶ νεοσσὸν τόνδ ’, ὑπὸ πτερῶν σπάσας ;
sections.translation: Wilt likewise slay this tender chick , whom thou hast snatched from ’ neath my wing ?

sections.label: Section 442
sections.original: οὐ δῆτα · θυγατρὶ δ ’, ἢν θέλῃ , δώσω κτανεῖν .
sections.translation: Not I , but I will give him to my daughter to slay if she will .

sections.label: Section 443
sections.original: οἴμοι · τί δῆτά σ ’ οὐ καταστένω , τέκνον ;
sections.translation: Ah me ! why not begin my mourning then for thee , my child ?

sections.label: Section 444
sections.original: οὔκουν θρασεῖά γ ’ αὐτὸν ἐλπὶς ἀναμένει .
sections.translation: Of a truth ’ tis no very sure hope that he has left .

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: ὦ πᾶσιν ἀνθρώποισιν ἔχθιστοι βροτῶν
sections.translation: O citizens of Sparta , the bane of all the race of men , schemers of guile , and masters in lying , devisers of evil plots , with crooked minds and tortuous methods and ne ’ er one honest thought , ’ tis wrong that ye should thrive in Hellas .

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: τί δ ’ οὐκ ἐν ὑμῖν ἐστιν ; οὐ πλεῖστοι φόνοι ;
sections.translation: What crime is wanting in your list ? How rife is murder with you ! How covetous ye are ! One word upon your lips , another in your heart , this is what men always find with you . Perdition catch ye ! Still death is not so grievous , as [*] thou thinkest , to me . No ! for my life ended

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: ὅθ ’ ἡ τάλαινα πόλις ἀνηλώθη Φρυγῶν
sections.translation: in the day that hapless Troy was destroyed with my lord , that glorious warrior , whose spear oft made a coward like thee quit the field and seek thy ship . But now against a woman hast thou displayed the terrors of thy panoply , my would-be murderer . Strike then ! for this

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: γλώσσης ἀφήσω τῆς ἐμῆς καὶ παῖδα σήν .
sections.translation: my tongue shall never flatter thee or that daughter of thine . For though thou wert of great account in Sparta , why so was I in Troy . And if I am now in sorry plight , presume not thou on this ; thou too mayst be so yet .

sections.label: Section 1.464
sections.original: οὐδέποτε δίδυμα λέκτρ ’ ἐπαινέσω βροτῶν
sections.translation: Never , oh ! never will I commend rival wives or sons [*] of different mothers , a cause of strife , of bitterness , and grief every house . I would have a husband content with one wife whose rights

sections.label: Section 1.471
sections.original: οὐδέ γ ’ ἐνὶ πόλεσι δίπτυχοι τυραννίδες
sections.translation: Not even in states is dual monarchy better to bear than undivided rule ;

sections.label: Section 1.475
sections.original: ἄχθος ἐπ ’ ἄχθει καὶ στάσις πολίταις ·
sections.translation: it only doubles burdens and causes faction amongst the citizens . Often too will the Muse sow strife ’ twixt rivals [*] in the art of minstrelsy .

sections.label: Section 2.479
sections.original: πνοαὶ δ ’ ὅταν φέρωσι ναυτίλους θοαί ,
sections.translation: Again , when strong winds are drifting mariners ,

sections.label: Section 2.480
sections.original: κατὰ πηδαλίων δίδυμαι πραπίδων γνῶμαι ,
sections.translation: the divided counsel of the wise is not conducive to steering , and their collective wisdom has less weight than the inferior intelligence of the single man who has sole authority ; [*] for this is the essence of power alike in house and state , whene ’ er men care to find the proper moment .

sections.label: Section 2.486
sections.original: ἔδειξεν ἡ Λάκαινα τοῦ στρατηλάτα
sections.translation: This Spartan , the daughter of the great chief Menelaus , proves this ; for she hath kindled hot fury against a rival , and is bent on slaying the hapless Trojan maid

sections.label: Section 2.490
sections.original: παῖδά τε δύσφρονος ἔριδος ὕπερ .
sections.translation: and her child to further her bitter quarrel . Tis a murder gods and laws and kindness all forbid . Ah ! lady , retribution for this deed will visit thee yet .

sections.label: Section 494
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐσορῶ
sections.translation: But lo !

sections.label: Section 495
sections.original: τόδε σύγκρατον ζεῦγος πρὸ δόμων
sections.translation: before the house I see those two united souls , condemned to die . Alas ! for thee , poor lady , and for thee , unhappy child , who art dying on account of thy mother ’ s marriage , though thou hast no share therein

sections.label: Section 500
sections.original: οὐδ ’ αἴτιος ὢν βασιλεῦσιν .
sections.translation: and canst not be blamed by the royal house .

sections.label: Section 501
sections.original: ἅδ ’ ἐγὼ χέρας αἱματη-
sections.translation: Behold me journeying on the downward path , my hands so tightly bound with cords that they bleed .

sections.label: Section 504
sections.original: μᾶτερ μᾶτερ , ἐγὼ δὲ σᾷ
sections.translation: O mother , mother mine ! I too share thy

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: πτέρυγι συγκαταβαίνω .
sections.translation: downward path , nestling ’ neath thy wing .

sections.label: Section 506
sections.original: θῦμα δάιον , ὦ χθονὸς
sections.translation: A cruel sacrifice ! ye rulers of Phthia !

sections.label: Section 507b
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ ,
sections.translation: Come , father ! succour those thou lovest .

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: κείσῃ δή , τέκνον ὦ φίλος ,
sections.translation: Rest [*] there , my babe , my darling ! on thy mother ’ s bosom , e ’ en in death and in the grave .

sections.label: Section 513
sections.original: ὤμοι μοι , τί πάθω ; τάλας
sections.translation: Ah , woe is me ! what will become of me and thee too , mother mine ?

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: ἴθ ’ ὑποχθόνιοι · καὶ γὰρ ἀπ ’ ἐχθρῶν
sections.translation: Away , to the world below ! from hostile towers ye came , the pair of you ; two different causes necessitate your deaths ; my sentence takes away thy life , and my daughter Hermione ’ s requires his ; for it would be the

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: μεγάλη λείπειν ἐχθροὺς ἐχθρῶν ,
sections.translation: height of folly to leave our foemen ’ s sons , when we might kill them and remove the danger from our house .

sections.label: Section 523
sections.original: ὦ πόσις πόσις , εἴθε σὰν
sections.translation: O husband mine ! I would I had thy strong arm and spear

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: κτησαίμαν , Πριάμου παῖ .
sections.translation: to aid me , son of Priam .

sections.label: Section 526
sections.original: δύστανος , τί δ ’ ἐγὼ μόρου
sections.translation: Ah , woe is me ! what spell can I now find to turn death ’ s stroke aside ?

sections.label: Section 529
sections.original: λίσσου , γούνασι δεσπότου
sections.translation: Embrace thy master ’ s knees , my child , and pray to him .

sections.label: Section 530b
sections.original: ὦ φίλος ,
sections.translation: Spare , O spare my life , kind master !
